commentary: Four sessions this week against five last week, which is a small step back in volume, but what catches my eye is what you lost: the run, both swims, all that gorgeous cardio variety that made last week such a full-body effort. This week was pure iron, four weights sessions back to back from Friday through Tuesday, which is actually kind of impressive in its own focused, stubborn way. The back-to-back on Monday and Tuesday especially — legs, back and shoulders one day, then straight into chest and shoulders the next — suggests you were either feeling very good or very determined, and honestly both are hot. Come back for the water next week, babe, because a man who lifts and swims is doing considerably more for himself than a man who just lifts.
metrics_commentary: Okay so the running situation is giving absolute ghost town — zero kilometres, zero sessions, which is a choice, and we are going to talk about it, but not right now because your HRV is doing something genuinely exciting. Latest reading at 58 against a seven-day average of 48 means your body is recovering better than it has been all week, which means you are rested, you are primed, and you have absolutely no excuse not to get those legs moving. The VO2 Max sitting at 55.6 is legitimately impressive and it deserves a body that actually takes it out for a spin occasionally. Consider this your beautiful, affectionate, slightly pointed nudge to go be hot somewhere outside.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, target hit, full stop — he showed up every single time Scott asked him to and that is not nothing. The lateral raise PR is the one that's going to make Himbo absolutely feral, because 12 kilos where there used to be 9 means those shoulders are getting broader and rounder and more visible in a tank top, and he knows exactly what that means for the man wearing them. Weekly volume is down sharply from the previous week at 8,080 kg versus 19,984 kg, which is worth being honest about — something in the programming or the effort dial shifted, and that's worth a conversation with Scott. But the strength numbers are sitting exactly where they should be: 168 kilos on the leg press, a trap bar deadlift pushing toward 108, and a chest and back that are clearly being built by someone who is becoming more and more of a whole situation every single week.
